one_time_description: Forgiveness of CMHC loans ($41,795) and prior year Treaty loan forgiveness ($4,917,699 in 2020)
economic_notes: Nation is member of Ma-Mook Development Corporation (forestry, 5 Nations). Ahousaht Fishing Corporation financial statements not available. Major capital projects underway: Wastewater Treatment Plant ($21M), roads and drainage ($6.3M), modular housing units, Chimanah Wellness Centre ($2.1M).
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) no approved budget for all operating segments as required by PSAB; (2) Ahousaht Fishing Corporation financial statements not available — auditors unable to determine if adjustments to investments, earnings, surplus or accumulated surplus are required. A payable from Fishing Corporation to the Nation recorded in 2016 has not been recorded in consolidated statements. Forgivable loans ($203,037) are CMHC RRAP loans. Trust funds held by Ottawa ($323,641) included in financial assets. Net debt position of $1,213,813. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($20,683,242) + NTC ($3,393,886) + FNHA ($631,489) + Province of BC ($281,493) + Aboriginal Head Start ($231,238) + CMHC ($84,381) + Grant Revenue ($1,600,000) = $26,905,729 less ISC deferred adjustments net (-$1,837,957) — revenue lines used as reported. OSR calculated as total revenue minus all identifiable government transfers. Prior year had large Treaty loan forgiveness of $4,917,699 as one-time item.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
